rationale: Title Y is more consistent with Europace survey-title style: concise, professional, and easy to parse. It identifies the condition, population, EHRA source, and practice-survey design without overloading the reader. Title X contains useful specifics, especially the sample size and treatment spectrum, but its long construction and phrases such as "first-line direct oral anticoagulant switching to last-resort appendage closure" make it cumbersome.
loser_weakness: Title X is overlong and syntactically heavy, reducing clarity and editorial appeal despite stronger specificity.
